statutory_basis: Federal Constitution Third Schedule (Election of YDPA)

rule_set.text_or_description: YDPA shall be elected by Conference of Rulers every five years from among the nine hereditary Rulers (Article 32, Third Schedule).
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Conference of Rulers vote; succession arithmetic codified in Third Schedule

rule_set.text_or_description: YDPA acts on advice of Cabinet or Minister, except where Constitution expressly grants discretion (Article 40(1)-(2)). Discretionary functions: appointment of PM, withholding consent to dissolution, requisition of Conference of Rulers.
Pardon power under Article 42 vested in YDPA, exercised on advice of Pardons Board which includes Attorney General and members nominated by YDPA.
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Pardons Board procedure; deliberations not published

rule_set.text_or_description: YDPA may proclaim Emergency on advice if satisfied a grave emergency exists threatening security, economic life, or public order. Court of Appeal has held proclamation/refusal non-reviewable (2026).
rule_set.legal_source: Federal Constitution Article 150; Court of Appeal ruling in Sri Ram interpretation
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Self-enforcing once proclaimed; revocation by YDPA or House resolution

primary_sources.title: Federal Constitution of Malaysia (as amended); Part IV; Third Schedule
Faruqi, S.S. Document of Destiny (Star Publications, 2008)

notes: Encodes the constitutional rules that the YDPA institutional actor operates under. Distinct from Conference of Rulers, which has separate institutional standing (Articles 38, 153). The legitimacy time series will be populated as events are encoded.
